questions: Which company has the highest ratio of employees to total annual revenue (employees divided by full-year revenue)?
Which company has the highest combined score of satisfaction rating plus average quarterly growth rate?
Among companies that had negative Q3 growth, which has the highest debt-to-equity ratio?
Looking at the jump from first-quarter to fourth-quarter revenue, which company saw the biggest gain?
Among companies with more than 26,833 employees, which has the lowest total annual revenue?
Looking at the gap between a company's best and worst quarters, which firm showed the most revenue volatility?
Restricting to firms that went public, which has the smallest D/E ratio?
Which firm is the second-oldest by founding date?
Which company had the highest average quarterly growth rate across all four quarters?
Which private company has the largest headcount?
constraints: Must include the headquarters city of the company that answers Question 4.
Must include the CEO's last name of the company that answers Question 9.
Must include the headquarters country of the company that answers Question 5.
Must include a prime number equal to nextPrime((employees of the answer to Question 4 mod 100) + 11), as digits.
Must include the equation "A+B=C" (digits, no spaces) where A=((Q1 revenue of the answer to Question 4 mod 90)+10) and B=((Q4 revenue of the answer to Question 5 mod 90)+10) and C=A+B.
Acrostic: the first letters of the first 8 words must spell the first 8 letters of (initials(Q4)+initials(Q9)+initials(Q5)+initials(Q2)), uppercased.
Tip: avoid extra punctuation-only "words"; words are split on spaces.
doc:
# THE GLOBAL BUSINESS CHRONICLE

Cryo Prime has long operated out of Thornfield, Halvesta; in bio logistics circles it's sometimes just called Cryo.

For Alta Pulse, in the Q1, revenue was 4,483 million dollars and it declined 22%; the the year-end quarter landed at just over 32 million dollars with increased 7%. The middle quarters were Q2 $2,825 million (contracted by 30 percent) and third quarter $4,705 million (expanded 35%).

Kova Core has long operated out of Yarmouth, Thalassa; in petro extraction circles it's sometimes just called Kova.

On leverage, ZM sits at 4.9 D/E; on morale, 1.8/10.
